news.title:
Tech Mahindra shares rose 4.7% to ₹1,404.70 despite Citi's 'sell' rating with a ₹1,260 target.
CLSA rated 'Outperform' with a ₹1,698 target, citing 21% upside potential.
news.summary:
Tech Mahindra shares rose 4.7% to ₹1,404.70 despite Citi's 'sell' rating with a ₹1,260 target.
CLSA rated 'Outperform' with a ₹1,698 target, citing 21% upside potential.

news.title:
Tech Mahindra's subsidiary to acquire 20% stake in Tech Mahindra Arabia.
The deal with Midad is valued at ₹2.06 billion.

news.title:

AI adoption challenges traditional IT billing, sparking shift to outcome-based models.

news.title:
TCS, Infosys, and Wipro saw market cap declines of 36%, 29%, and 42% respectively since 2021.
Tech Mahindra (+14%) and HCL Tech (+18%) improved market cap due to AI focus and diversified portfolios.
news.summary:

news.title:

Company focuses on productivity; launches AI platform with Microsoft for data modernization.

news.title:

news.title:
Tech Mahindra partners with Orange Business for a 5-year strategic global digital transformation, focusing on AI automation and secure platforms.

news.title:
Indian IT sector faces challenges with mid and small-cap IT stocks losing over a third of their value in six months, while Nifty IT index dropped 15%.
AI adoption may reshape the sector; Infosys, Tech Mahindra, and TCS pivot to AI strategies, but market-beating returns remain uncertain near-term.
news.summary:
Indian IT sector faces challenges with mid and small-cap IT stocks losing over a third of their value in six months, while Nifty IT index dropped 15%.
AI adoption may reshape the sector; Infosys, Tech Mahindra, and TCS pivot to AI strategies, but market-beating returns remain uncertain near-term.

news.title:
IT stocks lead recovery post Anthropic-triggered selloff; Nifty reclaims 25,500, Sensex gains 485.2 points to trade at 82,711.1.
Infosys, HCLTECH, TCS, TECHM top Nifty gainers; Dr. Reddy's, MAXHEALTH, SBI among losers; FIIs net sellers at ₹102.5 crore.
news.summary:
IT stocks lead recovery post Anthropic-triggered selloff; Nifty reclaims 25,500, Sensex gains 485.2 points to trade at 82,711.1.
Infosys, HCLTECH, TCS, TECHM top Nifty gainers; Dr. Reddy's, MAXHEALTH, SBI among losers; FIIs net sellers at ₹102.5 crore.

news.title:
CLSA cuts price targets for 8 IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, and Wipro, citing downside risks.
Despite cuts, CLSA remains optimistic, preferring Tech Mahindra, Infosys, Persistent Systems, and Coforge.
news.summary:
CLSA cuts price targets for 8 IT stocks, including Infosys, TCS, and Wipro, citing downside risks.
Despite cuts, CLSA remains optimistic, preferring Tech Mahindra, Infosys, Persistent Systems, and Coforge.

news.title:
GIFT Nifty signals a weak start; Nifty futures down 0.5% at 25,592.5. Sensex gained 479.95 points, closing at 83,294.66.
Top Nifty gainers: Adani Ports, Dr. Reddy's, Kotak Mahindra. IT sector fell 1.4%, PSU Bank index rose 1.3%; volatility persists.
news.summary:
